executive_summary.feasibility_rationale: 4 triggered MODA pathways and 4 AUTH BLOCKERs across 5 services with a 12-month constraint is achievable with an 8-person team using AI-accelerated tooling, but tight given the Cloud Native pathway complexity and no Kubernetes experience.
top_risks: No Kubernetes experience — EKS adoption requires significant training
AUTH-Q1 BLOCKER across 4 services — agents cannot be deployed until resolved
SOC2 and PCI-DSS compliance gates add validation overhead

modernization_work_streams.name: Move to Modern DevOps
modernization_work_streams.objective: Implement CI/CD pipelines, IaC with CDK, automated testing, and progressive delivery for all 3 services

tasks.description: Establish shared CI/CD pipeline templates using CDK Pipelines pattern
tasks.acceptance_criteria: CDK Pipeline construct library with reusable stages deployed

tasks.description: Create CI/CD pipeline for aws-microservices with CDK synth, test, and deploy stages
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Pipeline deploys on push with integration tests gate

tasks.description: Create CI/CD pipeline for local-monolith with container build and deploy
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Pipeline builds container and deploys to App Runner on push

tasks.description: Create CI/CD pipeline for unishop-monolith with build, test, and deploy
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Pipeline builds JAR and deploys with health check validation

tasks.description: Define unishop-monolith infrastructure in CDK (VPC, EC2/ECS, RDS, ALB)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: CDK stack deploys all existing infra, drift-free

tasks.description: Add integration test suites for all 3 services
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Each service has integration tests running in CI, >70% API coverage

tasks.description: Implement progressive delivery (canary/blue-green) for each pipeline
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Canary deployments with automatic rollback on alarm triggers

success_criteria: All 3 services have automated CI/CD pipelines
Integration tests pass in CI for all services

modernization_work_streams.name: Move to Cloud Native
modernization_work_streams.objective: Decompose monoliths into microservices deployed on EKS with event-driven architecture

tasks.description: Design microservice decomposition strategy for unishop-monolith (domain boundaries, data ownership, API contracts)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Documented decomposition plan with service boundaries, data ownership, and migration sequence

tasks.description: Design microservice decomposition strategy for local-monolith (domain boundaries, API contracts)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Documented decomposition plan with service boundaries and migration sequence

tasks.description: Extract first service from unishop-monolith (user/auth domain) with EKS deployment
tasks.acceptance_criteria: User service deployed on EKS, API parity verified, traffic routing operational

tasks.description: Extract second service from unishop-monolith (product catalog) with EKS deployment
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Product service on EKS, data migrated, integration tests pass

tasks.description: Extract order/basket services from unishop-monolith with event-driven patterns
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Order service on EKS using EventBridge for async workflows

tasks.description: Modernize local-monolith: extract services and deploy on EKS with async patterns
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Core services extracted and running on EKS with EventBridge integration

success_criteria: Both monoliths decomposed into independently deployable services
Services deployed on EKS with Graviton instances

modernization_work_streams.name: Move to Containers
modernization_work_streams.objective: Containerize unishop-monolith and deploy on EKS with Graviton instances

tasks.description: Provision EKS cluster with Graviton node groups, VPC, and CDK definitions
tasks.acceptance_criteria: EKS cluster running with Graviton nodes, GitOps (Flux) configured, monitoring baseline deployed

tasks.description: Create multi-arch Dockerfile for unishop-monolith (Java/Spring Boot)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Docker image builds for arm64, passes health checks, starts in < 30s

tasks.description: Create Helm charts and Kubernetes manifests for unishop-monolith deployment
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Helm chart deploys successfully on EKS, HPA configured, health checks passing

tasks.description: Migrate unishop-monolith traffic from EC2 to EKS with blue-green cutover
tasks.acceptance_criteria: 100% traffic on EKS, EC2 instances decommissioned, latency within 10% of baseline

success_criteria: unishop-monolith running on EKS with Graviton

modernization_work_streams.name: Move to Managed Databases
modernization_work_streams.objective: Migrate self-managed MySQL to Aurora PostgreSQL with automatic failover

tasks.description: Provision Aurora PostgreSQL cluster with CDK (Multi-AZ, Graviton, encryption with CMK)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Aurora cluster deployed with Multi-AZ, KMS CMK encryption, automated backups

tasks.description: Convert MySQL schema and queries to PostgreSQL, update data access layer
tasks.acceptance_criteria: All queries converted, MyBatis mappers updated, integration tests pass against Aurora

tasks.description: Execute data migration using DMS with validation and cutover
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Data migrated with zero loss validated, application pointing to Aurora, old MySQL decommissioned

agent_readiness_work_streams.name: Implement Agent Identity & Authorization
agent_readiness_work_streams.objective: Implement machine identity authentication, scoped permissions, audit logging, and identity suspension across all services

tasks.description: Design shared machine identity pattern: Cognito resource server with client_credentials and API Gateway IAM authorization
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Architecture decision record documenting chosen auth pattern, Cognito resource server with scopes defined

tasks.description: Implement machine identity for aws-microservices (API Gateway IAM authorizer + Cognito)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: All API Gateway methods require authentication, agent calls attributed to principal

tasks.description: Implement machine identity for books-api (Cognito client_credentials + API key)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: All endpoints require authentication, GET /books secured with API key

tasks.description: Implement machine identity for local-monolith (API key with principal attribution)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Bearer token auth alongside session auth, API calls logged with principal

tasks.description: Implement machine identity for unishop-monolith (OAuth2 client credentials via Spring Security)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Spring Security enforces auth on all endpoints, agent identities scoped

tasks.description: Implement scoped permissions (read-only vs read-write) across all services
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Agent identities have configurable scope, read-only by default

tasks.description: Deploy CloudTrail with immutable S3 storage and log file validation for audit
tasks.acceptance_criteria: CloudTrail trail active, S3 Object Lock enabled, log validation passing

tasks.description: Implement agent identity suspension mechanism (emergency revocation Lambda)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Lambda can disable any agent identity within 30 seconds, EventBridge trigger on anomaly

success_criteria: All 4 BLOCKER services have machine identity authentication
Scoped permissions enforce read-only by default

agent_readiness_work_streams.name: Ensure Transactional Integrity
agent_readiness_work_streams.objective: Implement rate limiting, circuit breakers, and compensation patterns across services

tasks.description: Implement API Gateway usage plans with per-key rate limits for aws-microservices and books-api
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Usage plans configured, 429 responses on threshold breach

tasks.description: Add WAF rate-based rules for local-monolith and unishop-monolith
tasks.acceptance_criteria: WAF rate rules active, blocking runaway request patterns

tasks.description: Add DLQ to aws-microservices SQS queue and configure Lambda timeouts
tasks.acceptance_criteria: DLQ configured, failed messages captured, Lambda timeouts set

tasks.description: Implement saga pattern for local-monolith fulfillment workflow
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Each fulfillment step has compensating action, partial failures roll back cleanly

tasks.description: Add compensation endpoints for unishop-monolith basket operations
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Undo/cancel operations available, multi-step failures handled gracefully

tasks.description: Add DELETE /books/{isbn} endpoint with soft-delete for books-api
tasks.acceptance_criteria: DELETE endpoint operational, soft-delete with status field

success_criteria: Rate limiting active on all 4 services
Compensation patterns implemented for write workflows

agent_readiness_work_streams.name: Improve Data Accessibility
agent_readiness_work_streams.objective: Implement PII filtering, pagination, input validation, and data residency controls

tasks.description: Implement agent-specific response DTOs excluding PII for all 3 application services
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Agent-scoped endpoints return only non-PII fields

tasks.description: Add cursor-based pagination to all list endpoints across 4 services
tasks.acceptance_criteria: All list endpoints support ?limit=&cursor= parameters, default limit 50

tasks.description: Implement structured input validation with field-level error responses
tasks.acceptance_criteria: 400 responses include field name, constraint, and structured error code

tasks.description: Implement PII redaction in CloudWatch Logs (data protection policies)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: CloudWatch Logs data protection active, PII masked in logs

tasks.description: Document data residency requirements and pin services to specific regions
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Data residency policy documented, CDK stacks pinned to region

agent_readiness_work_streams.name: Add Agent Observability
agent_readiness_work_streams.objective: Implement distributed tracing, structured logging, and alerting across all services

tasks.description: Enable X-Ray tracing and structured JSON logging across all Lambda services
tasks.acceptance_criteria: X-Ray traces end-to-end, structured logs with correlation_id

tasks.description: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector on EKS for container services
tasks.acceptance_criteria: ADOT DaemonSet running, traces flowing to X-Ray

tasks.description: Add CloudWatch Alarms for error rates, P99 latency, and agent-specific metrics
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Alarms for 5xx rate > 1%, P99 > 2s, SNS notifications configured

success_criteria: Distributed tracing operational across all services
Alerting on error rates and latency

cross_dimension_dependencies.rationale: Modern DevOps CI/CD pipeline required for deploying observability instrumentation reliably
Container infrastructure (EKS) must be established before cloud-native decomposition can deploy services
Machine identity must exist before agent-specific data access controls can scope responses by caller identity

phases.name: Foundation & Safety
phases.description: DevOps foundation, managed database migration, agent identity (BLOCKER resolution), rate limiting

phases.name: Core Execution
phases.description: Containerization, cloud-native decomposition, data accessibility, observability

phases.name: Optimization & Validation
phases.description: Final validation, compliance audit, agent pilot testing, handover documentation

milestones.name: DevOps Foundation Complete
milestones.criteria: CI/CD pipelines operational for all 3 services

milestones.name: Critical BLOCKERs Resolved
milestones.criteria: All AUTH-Q1 BLOCKERs remediated, agents can authenticate

milestones.name: Database Migration Complete
milestones.criteria: Aurora PostgreSQL operational, old MySQL decommissioned

milestones.name: EKS Platform Ready
milestones.criteria: EKS cluster running with Graviton, first service deployed

milestones.name: Agent Pilot Ready
milestones.criteria: First service passes full ARA re-evaluation with no BLOCKERs

milestones.name: Cloud Native Decomposition Complete
milestones.criteria: Both monoliths decomposed, all services on EKS

milestones.name: Portfolio Execution Complete
milestones.criteria: All work streams complete, exit criteria met

timeline.parallelization_strategy: moderate — independent streams (WS-04, WS-05, WS-06) run in parallel with WS-01; dependent streams (WS-02 depends on WS-01+WS-03) run sequentially

risk_register.description: No Kubernetes experience on the team creates steep learning curve for EKS adoption
risk_register.mitigation: Allocate 2 weeks for EKS/K8s training before WS-03 begins. Consider AWS EKS Blueprints for accelerated setup.
risk_register.contingency: Use ECS Fargate as a simpler container orchestrator if EKS adoption stalls
risk_register.trigger: Team velocity on EKS tasks falls below 50% of estimates

risk_register.description: AUTH-Q1 BLOCKERs across 4 services mean agents cannot be safely deployed until identity is implemented
risk_register.mitigation: Prioritize WS-05 Phase 1 in Foundation phase. No agent deployment until machine identity operational.
risk_register.contingency: Deploy read-only agent proxy with hardcoded API keys as interim measure
risk_register.trigger: WS-05 Phase 1 not complete by week 8

risk_register.description: 12-month constraint may be tight given 42-week expected AI-accelerated timeline plus compliance overhead
risk_register.mitigation: Monitor velocity weekly. Identify scope reduction candidates (defer WS-02 Phase 2 if behind).
risk_register.contingency: Reduce cloud-native scope to single monolith decomposition, defer second monolith
risk_register.trigger: Behind schedule by >2 weeks at week 16 checkpoint

risk_register.description: SOC2 and PCI-DSS compliance requirements add validation gates during container and database migrations
risk_register.mitigation: Engage compliance team early. Maintain dual-running during cutovers for audit continuity.
risk_register.contingency: Extend dual-running periods with automated compliance validation scripts
risk_register.trigger: Compliance review identifies blocking findings during migration

risk_register.description: 8-person team spread across 8 work streams may reduce velocity per stream
risk_register.mitigation: Assign dedicated pairs per work stream phase. Use moderate parallelization — max 3 concurrent streams.
risk_register.contingency: Serialize work streams more aggressively if context-switching overhead exceeds 20%
risk_register.trigger: Sprint velocity drops below 70% of initial velocity for 2 consecutive sprints

risk_register.description: Budget of $1.2M may be tight given dual-running costs during migration and EKS infrastructure
risk_register.mitigation: Use Graviton instances (20% cost reduction). Minimize dual-running periods. Track burn rate monthly.
risk_register.contingency: Reduce EKS node groups, use Spot instances for non-production workloads
risk_register.trigger: Monthly burn rate exceeds budget projection by >15%

risk_register.description: Cross-dimension dependencies between MODA and ARA may extend critical path beyond single-dimension estimates
risk_register.mitigation: Track cross-dimension dependency resolution in weekly sync. Identify parallel paths within dependent streams.
risk_register.contingency: Decouple ARA work from MODA by implementing auth patterns on existing architecture first
risk_register.trigger: Cross-dimension dependency blocks a work stream for >1 week

notes.ai_tooling: $500/month per engineer for AI coding assistants
notes.training_breakdown: $15K EKS/K8s training + $15K CDK/IaC training + $15K DevOps practices + $10K agent-readiness patterns + $10K compliance training

task_category_distribution.high_acceleration_40_60_pct: 65% of total effort (IaC, CI/CD, code migration, test generation, API specs)
task_category_distribution.medium_acceleration_15_30_pct: 15% of total effort (data migration validation, security implementation)
task_category_distribution.minimal_acceleration_0_10_pct: 20% of total effort (architecture design, production cutover, compliance review, training)

lagging_indicators_moda: Deployment frequency (target: daily for each service)
lagging_indicators_ara: ARA re-score improvement (target: all services Pilot-Ready or better)
Mean time to agent identity suspension (target: < 60 seconds)
exit_criteria: No High-severity gaps remaining in MODA or ARA re-evaluation
All services have CI/CD pipelines with integration tests
All services achieve Pilot-Ready or better on ARA re-evaluation

assumptions: Team availability at 70% capacity for execution (30% for BAU, on-call, meetings)
No major production incidents requiring sustained attention during migration
MODA and ARA reports are current (assessed 2026-05-18, within 90-day freshness window)
Infrastructure and tooling budget of $1.2M approved and available
Agent deployment does not proceed until BLOCKER remediation completes (WS-05 Phase 1)
AI tooling available to engineers (coding assistants, agent-based code generation, automated test generation)
AI acceleration estimates based on 2025-2026 tooling capabilities; actual acceleration varies by team proficiency
AWS account permissions and sandbox environments available for team
Compliance team available for SOC2/PCI-DSS review within 2-week SLA
EKS cluster costs covered by existing AWS commitment or included in $1.2M budget
Existing Jenkins CI/CD will be replaced (not maintained in parallel long-term)

decision_points.question: What container orchestrator should be used for modernized services?
options: ECS Fargate (simpler, no K8s experience needed)
decision_points.recommendation: EKS with Graviton — aligns with preferences, 20% cost savings, team training included in plan

decision_points.question: What database engine should replace self-managed MySQL for unishop-monolith?
decision_points.recommendation: Aurora PostgreSQL — better cloud-native ecosystem, aligns with preferences, superior performance for complex queries

decision_points.question: What machine identity approach should be used for agent authentication?
options: Cognito with client_credentials + API Gateway authorizer
API Gateway IAM authorization with SigV4
Custom JWT with shared signing key
decision_points.recommendation: Cognito with client_credentials — provides identity lifecycle management, scopes, and revocation out of the box

decision_points.question: Should cloud-native decomposition be incremental (strangler fig) or big-bang?
decision_points.recommendation: Phased approach: containerize monolith first (WS-03), then incrementally extract services (WS-02). Reduces risk and maintains availability.

decision_points.question: What IaC tool should be standardized across the portfolio?
decision_points.recommendation: AWS CDK (TypeScript) — aligns with preferences, team has TypeScript experience from aws-microservices, native AWS constructs

decision_points.question: When can agents begin calling services?
options: After WS-05 Phase 1 (week 8) — read-only with machine identity
After WS-05 + WS-06 complete (week 14) — with rate limiting
After full remediation (week 34) — all RISK-SAFETY addressed
decision_points.recommendation: After WS-05 Phase 1 + WS-06 Phase 1 (week 10) — machine identity + rate limiting provides minimum safety for read-only agents

decision_points.question: What training approach should be used for the team's Kubernetes gap?
options: Embedded AWS SA support during first EKS deployment
decision_points.recommendation: Combination: 3-day instructor-led training + embedded AWS SA for first 4 weeks of EKS work

decision_points.question: Should local-monolith be rewritten in Java/Spring or kept as PHP for cloud-native?
options: Keep PHP with modern framework (Laravel/Symfony) on containers
decision_points.recommendation: Keep PHP on containers initially (WS-03 scope), then evaluate rewrite as part of WS-02 decomposition based on velocity and maintainability
